SEARCH
Package server.scheduler

Class SchedulerService

java.lang.Object
server.scheduler.SchedulerService
public class SchedulerService extends Object
Runs the registered SchedulerJobs on a shared ScheduledExecutorService, polling every SchedulerConfig.getPollSeconds() seconds. Owned by OrderServer: start() is called on server startup and shutdown() on server stop, so the threads live exactly as long as the server and never leak.

Exception isolation. Every job run goes through safeRun(server.scheduler.SchedulerJob, boolean), which swallows and logs any exception. A scheduleAtFixedRate task that throws is silently cancelled by the JDK and would never run again; containing the throw keeps every job alive across a one-off failure (a transient DB blip, say).

Manual triggers run in force mode. runNow(String) and runAllNow() submit an off-cycle run to the same executor — the server console wires its "run now" buttons to these for the live defense. Those manual runs call the job in force mode (SchedulerJob.runOnce(boolean) with true), so the job acts on every eligible row immediately rather than only those past its real time threshold. The scheduled start() runs stay non-forced and respect the configured 24h/2h/1h windows.

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private final ScheduledExecutorService
    executor
    Stores the executor value used by this component.
    private final Map<String,SchedulerJob>
    jobs
    Insertion-ordered so the console lists jobs in a stable order.
    private final Consumer<String>
    log
    One-line summary sink, forwarded to each job and used for lifecycle logging.
    private final long
    pollSeconds
    How often each job re-runs, captured once from SchedulerConfig.
    private boolean
    started
    Stores the started value used by this component.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    private static ThreadFactory
    daemonThreads()
    Names the threads and marks them daemon so a missed shutdown() can never keep the JVM alive.

    The names of the registered jobs, in registration order — used by the server console to build one trigger control per job.

    Performs the register operation.

    Triggers an off-cycle run of every registered job.

    Triggers one off-cycle run of the named job, off the caller's thread (so an FX button handler does not block).

    Runs a job, containing any exception so the executor (and the job's recurring schedule) survives a one-off failure.

    shutdown()
    Stops polling and shuts the executor down, waiting briefly for in-flight runs to finish.
    void

    Schedules every registered job at the configured fixed interval.

  • Field Details

    • jobs

      private final Map<String,SchedulerJob> jobs
      Insertion-ordered so the console lists jobs in a stable order.

    • log

      private final Consumer<String> log
      One-line summary sink, forwarded to each job and used for lifecycle logging.

    • pollSeconds

      private final long pollSeconds
      How often each job re-runs, captured once from SchedulerConfig.

    • executor

      private final ScheduledExecutorService executor
      Stores the executor value used by this component.

    • started

      private volatile boolean started
      Stores the started value used by this component.

  • Constructor Details

    • SchedulerService

      public SchedulerService(Consumer<String> log)
      Builds the service and registers the jobs this session ships. The jobs share the supplied log sink so their one-line summaries land in the server console's activity log.
      Parameters:
      log - sink for scheduler/job summary lines (e.g. listener::onLog)

  • Method Details

    • register

      private void register(SchedulerJob job)
      Performs the register operation.
      Parameters:
      job - value supplied to the operation

    • start

      public void start()
      Schedules every registered job at the configured fixed interval. Idempotent — a second call is ignored.

    • runNow

      public void runNow(String jobName)
      Triggers one off-cycle run of the named job, off the caller's thread (so an FX button handler does not block). A no-op with a log line if the name is unknown. The run is in force mode (see the class javadoc): a manual trigger drops the job's time threshold so its effect lands immediately.
      Parameters:
      jobName - the job's SchedulerJob.name()

    • runAllNow

      public void runAllNow()
      Triggers an off-cycle run of every registered job.

    • jobNames

      public Set<String> jobNames()
      The names of the registered jobs, in registration order — used by the server console to build one trigger control per job.
      Returns:
      the job names

    • safeRun

      private void safeRun(SchedulerJob job, boolean force)
      Runs a job, containing any exception so the executor (and the job's recurring schedule) survives a one-off failure.
      Parameters:
      force - true for a manual forced run (drop the time threshold), false for a normal scheduled sweep

    • shutdown

      public void shutdown()
      Stops polling and shuts the executor down, waiting briefly for in-flight runs to finish. Called from OrderServer.stop() so no scheduler threads outlive the server.

    • daemonThreads

      private static ThreadFactory daemonThreads()
      Names the threads and marks them daemon so a missed shutdown() can never keep the JVM alive.
